briefing.opening: Hello everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for July 17, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Extreme Weather Events Sweeping the Globe and Climate Crisis Warnings
sections.content: News reported today clearly demonstrates that the crisis due to climate change is intensifying globally. Domestically, despite being the monsoon season, drought has intensified in some regions, with the Unmun Dam entering the 'severe' stage [4]. Climate change has also led to a crisis in Jeju's seas, causing the aquaculture industry to face an 'emergency' [5]. Furthermore, heatwaves are being defined as 'climate murder,' and a reorganization of response measures has been announced [6], indicating the severe impact of extreme weather domestically. Internationally, Wisconsin and Chicago areas were covered in wildfire smoke [22, 23, 35], which is analyzed as a phenomenon linked to climate change [23, 31]. In particular, the Australian Bureau of Meteorology warned that the El Niño phenomenon could be the strongest on record, pointing out its potential link to record global temperatures [43].

sections.title: 🌱 Efforts Towards Renewable Energy Transition and Building Efficient Energy Systems
sections.content: Positive progress is being observed in the renewable energy sector, which is central to addressing the climate crisis. To resolve the challenge of efficiently utilizing excess renewable energy generation due to limitations in the existing grid, KEPCO KDN is addressing connection difficulties through its 'Distribution Grid ESS Construction Project' [12, 13, 16, 20]. Furthermore, Gochang Renewable and 3JCNS are introducing AI and digital transformation technologies into the renewable energy sector for full lifecycle management [14], with increased efficiency expected through technological innovation. Good Energy has signed its largest-ever renewable energy supply contract with Ørsted [17], and SSE increased its network investment by 83% in the first quarter and expanded its renewable energy generation [18], indicating continued growth in the renewable energy industry. These efforts align with the argument that, in the era of carbon neutrality, the energy paradigm must undergo a major shift from a 'supply'-centric model to one focused on 'efficiency' and 'distributed systems' [7].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Spreading Climate Action and Increasing Citizen Participation Domestically and Internationally
sections.content: Participation and action from various sectors of society are active in responding to the climate crisis. Domestically, Mapo-gu Mayor Yoo Dong-gyun launched the 'Plant 5 Million Trees Again' campaign to address climate change [3]. In Siheung City, the Climate Citizen Network was launched, establishing a carbon neutrality communication system with citizens [8]. Religious communities are also taking the lead in addressing the climate crisis, achieving National Determined Contributions (NDCs), and realizing carbon neutrality [9]. The Hamyang-gun Federation of Rural Leaders is carrying out empty pesticide bottle collection as part of carbon neutrality efforts [10], demonstrating continued action from various stakeholders. Internationally, a VR documentary exploring the lives of climate refugees in the South Pacific premiered at the Venice Film Festival, highlighting the human aspect of the climate crisis [2]. Meanwhile, efforts to restore climate information accessibility have been observed, such as the revival of a U.S. climate-related website, which had been shut down by the Trump administration, through the efforts of former employees [1].

sections.title: 🌍 Reaffirming the Importance of Climate Science and Policy Challenges
sections.content: The scientific link between climate change and extreme weather events is becoming increasingly clear [25]. A National Academy of Sciences report indicated an improving understanding of when climate change should be blamed [24, 34], providing crucial evidence for policy decisions. However, politicians appear to hold more polarized views on climate change than their constituents [26], suggesting that political barriers still exist in advancing climate policies. In particular, research indicating that climate change can reshape waterborne disease risks and hinder mitigation efforts [36, 42] warns of the complex impacts of the climate crisis on human health [41] and emphasizes the urgent need for a public health response.

briefing.closing: Today's briefing was an opportunity to review the reality of the deepening global climate crisis, along with the various efforts and challenges to overcome it. This is a time when continuous scientific insight and global cooperation are becoming even more crucial. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.title: Underground Walkway 'City Star Mall' Offers Refuge from Heat and Monsoon, Connecting City Hall Station to Dongdaemun History & Culture Park Station
articles.original_title: “더위·장마 피하자”시청역-동대문역사문화공원역까지 이어진 지하보도 ‘시티스타몰’ [정동길 옆 사진관]
articles.description: On July 17th, during the Constitution Day holiday and coinciding with the monsoon season, citizens and tourists were observed utilizing the 'City Star Mall,' an underground walkway extending from Seoul City Hall Station to Dongdaemun History & Culture Park. Despite the onset of the holiday, the region continued to experience adverse monsoon weather and persistent heatwaves. The 'City Star Mall' in central Seoul served as a refuge for both local residents and international visitors seeking respite from the heat while exploring the city.

summary.phenomenon: On July 17th, during the Constitution Day holiday, the 'City Star Mall,' an underground walkway connecting Seoul City Hall Station to Dongdaemun History & Culture Park, saw a significant influx of citizens and tourists. This movement was primarily driven by the desire to escape the adverse monsoon weather and the ongoing severe heatwave.

articles.original_description: 장마와 제헌절 연휴가 겹친 17일 서울 시청역에서 동대문역사문화공원까지 이어진 지하보도 ‘시티스타몰’에서 시민과 관광객들이 발걸음을 옮기고 있다. 문재원 기자제헌절 연휴가 시작된 17일에도 장마철 궂은 날씨와 폭염이 연일 기승을 부리고 있다.이날 서울 도심 한복판의 지하보도 ‘시티스타몰’에는 더위를 피해 서울 산책에 나선 시민들과 외국인 관광객들의 발길이 ···

articles.original_title: ‘We are waiting with bated breath’: experts alarmed as BoM says gathering El Niño could be strongest on record
articles.description: Most Australian capital cities have at least 80% chance of unusually warm and dry spring as climatologists watch developing system with increasing alarmFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastThe El Niño climate phenomenon linked to record global temperatures and now locked in place in the Pacific Ocean could develop into the strongest on record, according to Australia’s Bureau of Meteorology.Climatologists are watching the

summary.phenomenon: According to Australia's Bureau of Meteorology, the El Niño climate phenomenon, now locked in place in the Pacific Ocean, could develop into the strongest on record. This phenomenon is linked to record global temperatures.
summary.outlook: Consequently, most Australian capital cities have at least an 80% chance of an unusually warm and dry spring.

articles.title: As the UK and Europe battle deadly wildfires, what lessons can Australia offer?
articles.description: Knowledge learned over more than a century in Australia is being tested by worsening fires. It’s a familiar narrative around the worldThe violent hot red flames of deadly wildfires across the UK and Europe and scenes of panicked communities fleeing homes could not, at least geographically, be further away for Jan Harris.But sitting in her new home at Reedy Swamp in rural New South Wales in Australia, the 67-year-old has found herself in tears. Continue reading...

summary.phenomenon: Deadly wildfires are occurring across the UK and Europe, leading to communities fleeing their homes.
